Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.model.player

Class Player

java.lang.Object
com.example.battleship.model.player.Player
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
Direct Known Subclasses:
HumanPlayer, MachinePlayer
public abstract class Player extends Object implements Serializable
A participant in a game: a nickname and the Board holding their own fleet (the board the opponent shoots at).

Kept abstract so HumanPlayer and MachinePlayer can each add exactly what makes them different -- a machine additionally needs a ShootingStrategy to decide where to fire, while a human doesn't need any extra state at all, since their shots come straight from mouse clicks handled by the controller.

See Also:

    • hasLost

      public boolean hasLost()
      Returns:
      true once this player's entire fleet has been sunk
